channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: El auge y declive del AK-47
item.description: Una familia de armas que en su día fue omnipresente en el mercado estadounidense ha empezado a desaparecer por diversas razones.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Gibbons-Neff

media:content.media:credit: Adriana Zehbrauskas para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Jim Fuller, quien fabrica rifles estilo AK-47, en su oficina en Scottsdale, Arizona.

item.title: ¿Comer sano? No, ellos comen bíblicamente
item.description: Una dieta inspirada en las Escrituras ha encontrado nuevas audiencias en internet como sustento al movimiento de Hagamos a Estados Unidos saludable de nuevo.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher

item.title: Xi Says U.S.-China Relations Could Turn ‘Dangerous’ Over Taiwan
item.description: China’s leader, Xi Jinping said relations between the two countries could enter an “extremely dangerous place” if Mr. Trump ignored China’s demands over Taiwan.
item.dc:creator: Chris Buckley

media:content.media:description: Patriot missile systems on display during a Taiwanese military exercise in Taipei last year.

item.title: Picture Prompt | ‘Rots Your Brain’
item.description: What do you think this image is communicating?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

media:content.media:credit: Madison Swart for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Demonstrators rallied in April against the use of generative artificial intelligence in New York City schools.

item.title: What Could One Banana Cost? $10? Maybe for You, Some Fear.
item.description: Grocery stores can use shoppers’ personal data to charge different customers different prices. Lawmakers in New York are considering a ban.
item.dc:creator: Claire Fahy
item.category: Retail, Wholesale and Department Store Union

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for May 14, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: In a City of Big Dreams, Many Young Adults See a Cloudy Future
item.description: A bleak job market. Rising rents. Huge debt. In New York and other cities, traditional milestones of adulthood feel further away for some 20- and 30-year-olds.
item.dc:creator: Troy Closson

media:content.media:credit: Tessa Belle Dillman for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Soban Ali moved to New York eager to make his way in a new city. Like so many other young adults, he discovered hardship and disappointment.

item.title: It’s a Hard Time to be a Young Adult. We Want to Hear From You.
item.description: I’m a Times reporter writing about young people in the New York City area. Whether you’re a young adult, or work with them, your insights can help.
item.dc:creator: Troy Closson

media:content.media:credit: Karsten Moran for The New York Times

item.title: Inside Trump’s Summit Tour in Beijing: From Temple of Heaven to the Great Hall
item.description: The Temple of Heaven and Great Hall of the People embody distinct parts of China’s history, which Xi Jinping has sought to underscore while hosting state visits.
item.dc:creator: John Yoon and Joy Dong
item.category: Great Hall of the People (Beijing, China)

media:content.media:description: Mr. Trump and Mr. Xi at the Temple of Heaven in Beijing on Thursday.

item.title: Dejar la casa atrás para vivir sobre ruedas
item.description: Con pocos motivos para quedarse atados a un hogar fijo, algunos padres con el nido vacío están optando por salir a la carretera.
item.dc:creator: Tara Loader Wilkinson

media:content.media:credit: Lindsay D'Addato para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Kati y Vernon Evans vivieron nueve años en una casa rodante con sus cuatro hijos, y los tres últimos como padres con el nido vacío.

item.title: ¿D’Artagnan fue enterrado en los Países Bajos?
item.description: El pueblo de Wolder exhumó un esqueleto que estaba debajo de una de sus iglesias. Espera ansioso los resultados de ADN para saber si los restos pertenecen al conde inmortalizado por Alejandro Dumas.
item.dc:creator: Jeanna Smialek
item.category: Artagnan, Chas de Baatz, Castelmore, D'

media:content.media:description: Una excavación en la iglesia de San Pedro y San Pablo en Wolder, Países Bajos, para ver si los restos encontrados en la iglesia pertenecen al conde D’Artagnan, quien fue dramatizado, ficcionalizado e inmortalizado en Los tres mosqueteros de Dumas.

item.title: Palabra del día en inglés: ‘epithet’
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 35 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Word of the Day: epithet
item.description: This word has appeared in 35 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Stephen Colbert Slams ‘Trump’s Fabulous Billionaire Boys Trip’
item.description: Late night hosts responded to President Trump’s arrival in Beijing on Wednesday, accompanied by 17 chief executives including Tim Cook and Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)
The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: Honda Posts First Ever Annual Loss After Pullback From E.V.s
item.description: Honda posted its first loss since 1957 as it took a multibillion-dollar hit from scaling back its electric-vehicle plans.
item.dc:creator: River Akira Davis

media:content.media:description: A Honda dealership in Osaka, Japan.

item.title: En su visita a China, Trump no debe darle más victorias a Pekín
item.description: La política de Trump hacia China ha debilitado a EE. UU., especialmente en su segundo mandato.
item.dc:creator: El Comité Editorial

media:content.media:credit: Illustration by Rebecca Chew/The New York Times

item.title: Los países del Golfo van tras los chiíes mientras no cede la guerra en Irán
item.description: Decenas de ciudadanos del golfo Pérsico han sido acusados de pertenecer a células terroristas vinculadas a Irán mientras la guerra acelera un giro hacia un autoritarismo más profundo en la región.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Una columna de humo tras el ataque de un avión no tripulado en la ciudad de Kuwait en marzo.

item.title: 7 movimientos para prevenir el dolor de hombro
item.description: Estos ejercicios pueden ayudarte a evitar molestias al mejorar tu fuerza y movilidad.
item.dc:creator: Christine Yu and Theodore Tae

item.title: President Trump Goes to China
item.description: His talks with Xi Jinping in Beijing could decide whether the United States and China maintain their uneasy truce on trade.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: Great Hall of the People (Beijing, China)

media:content.media:description: President Trump and China’s leader, Xi Jinping, visiting the Temple of Heaven in Beijing on Thursday.

item.title: Shakira, Madonna and BTS Are First World Cup Final Halftime Show
item.description: The previous World Cup final drew more than 500 million live viewers. This year’s matchup is scheduled for July 19 at MetLife Stadium.
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Morgan

media:content.media:description: Shakira recorded the official World Cup song, “Dai Dai,” which features the Afrobeats star Burna Boy.

item.title: Midsize Cities Held Steady as U.S. Population Growth Declined
item.description: New census estimates show that the nation’s largest cities saw the sharpest slowdowns in population growth over the past year amid declines in immigration and plunging birthrates.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra

media:content.media:credit: Stacy Sodolak for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Austin, Texas, grew to have one million residents, but overall, the growth of midsize cities outpaced that of the larger ones near them.

item.title: Pedro Sánchez, de España, es némessis de Trump. Y está ganando
item.description: El líder de España ha establecido una filosofía de gobierno alternativa al trumpismo.
item.dc:creator: Omar G. Encarnación

item.title: Corrections: May 14, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Thursday, May 14, 2026.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Teenagers Get a Fresh Kick Out of Playing an Old Game
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Thursday, May 14, 2026.

item.title: Beijing Tightens Security As Trump and Xi Meet in China
item.description: Historic sites and roads have been closed in the Chinese capital for President Trump’s state visit, resulting in severe traffic and disappointed tourists.
item.dc:creator: Francesca Regalado and Murphy Zhao

media:content.media:description: Welcome ceremony for U.S. President Donald Trump in Beijing.

item.title: South Carolina Governor Plans Special Session to Redraw House Maps
item.description: Gov. Henry McMaster, a Republican, appears prepared to thrust the state into the nation’s redistricting wars.
item.dc:creator: Eduardo Medina

media:content.media:description: Gov. Henry McMaster’s decision to call back the state legislature came a day after the State Senate voted to block a resolution to do so.

item.title: Por qué Xi no necesita un acuerdo con Trump
item.description: China quiere que continúe la tregua comercial con EE. UU. Eso significa que Xi negociará con Trump para que no se impongan más aranceles, ni controles a las exportaciones, ni sanciones a sus empresas.
item.dc:creator: Lily Kuo
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas

media:content.media:credit: Foto de consorcio por Maxim Shemetov
media:content.media:description: El presidente de China, Xi Jinping, ha intentado redefinir los términos de la relación entre China y Estados Unidos.

item.title: U.S. Cruise Passenger Moved Out of Isolation
item.description: The passenger, who had tested positive once in the Canary Islands, was being monitored in a special biocontainment facility in Omaha, until a new test came back negative.
item.dc:creator: Sonia A. Rao and Apoorva Mandavilli
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

media:content.media:description: American passengers from the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship were being monitored at the Nebraska Medical Center in Omaha.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for May 14, 2026
item.description: Thirsty for a Thursday challenge? Simeon Seigel is here to help.
item.dc:creator: Sean McGowan

media:content.media:credit: Zinyange Auntony for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Time to break this Thursday puzzle wide open.

item.title: Is America Still No. 1?
item.description: The billionaire investor who predicted the 2008 financial crash is bearish on America’s future standing in the world. On “Interesting Times,” Ray Dalio tells the columnist Ross Douthat why.
item.dc:creator: Interesting Times

item.title: Watch Live: Trump Meets Xi as U.S.-China Summit Kicks Off
item.description: China’s leader, Xi Jinping, and President Trump participate in a meeting at Beijing’s Great Hall of the People.

item.title: Judge Orders U.S. to Return Colombian Woman Deported to Congo
item.description: The unusual ruling came after the judge found that the Trump administration had most likely violated the law by deporting the 55-year-old woman to the African country despite its refusal to take her.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar

media:content.media:description: The Colombian woman and other migrants are housed in a hotel outside Kinshasa, the capital of the Democratic Republic of Congo.

item.title: 11 Rescued From Raft After Plane Crash Off the Coast of Florida
item.description: Those on board, who were all Bahamian adults, spent about five hours stranded on a raft before rescuers hoisted them to safety. All were in stable condition, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Ziegler
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: Eleven people survived a plane crash on Tuesday off the coast of Melbourne, Fla., and clung to a raft for several hours before rescuers arrived, the authorities said.

item.title: Justice Dept. Sues D.C. Bar Over Efforts to Discipline Government Lawyers
item.description: The move escalates the administration’s feud with legal disciplinary bodies over the ethical conduct of government lawyers.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett

media:content.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Todd Blanche, the acting attorney general, accused the D.C. Bar, which handles complaints of lawyer misconduct, of acting as a “blatantly partisan arm of leftist causes.”

item.title: Trump’s Entourage to China Has Numerous Industry Executives
item.description: President Trump brought more than a dozen executives of lead financial and tech companies on his trip to Beijing. Trump is expected to discuss the creation of a board of investment and a board of trade with China.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Leventhal

item.title: Man Pleads Guilty to Ramming Car Into Chabad Headquarters
item.description: The man, Dan Sohail, admitted on Wednesday in Brooklyn federal court to pounding his car repeatedly into the building at the Chabad-Lubavitch Orthodox complex.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Bensimon and Santul Nerkar

media:content.media:credit: Dakota Santiago for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Dan Sohail admitted to pounding his gray Honda sedan into the wooden doors of the global headquarters of the Chabad-Lubavitch Orthodox Jewish movement in January.

item.title: Mamdani Urges State to Block Western Union’s Deal for Intermex
item.description: New York City’s mayor said the company’s proposed acquisition of Intermex could make it costlier for immigrants to send money abroad.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Hirsch

media:content.media:credit: Angelina Katsanis for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani of New York sent a state agency a letter asking it to block Western Union’s proposed $500 million acquisition of Intermex.

item.title: Why Does China Want Taiwan?
item.description: There are few issues in diplomacy more complicated than the status of the self-governing island, which China claims as its own. It is almost certain to come up when President Trump meets China’s leader.
item.dc:creator: Chris Buckley

media:content.media:credit: Lam Yik Fei for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Taiwan is certain to be raised by China’s leader, Xi Jinping, when he hosts President Trump this week.

item.title: Denise Powell Wins Democratic Primary in Key Nebraska House Race
item.description: Denise Powell prevailed in a close primary race for an Omaha-based seat, currently held by a retiring Republican, that could help determine control of the House.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning

media:content.media:description: Denise Powell narrowly won a Democratic House primary that featured an unusual argument: that electing her chief party rival, State Senator John Cavanaugh, could make it easier for Republicans to win the White House in 2028.

item.title: Activists, Lawmakers Urge Trump to Call for Release of Jimmy Lai and Dong Yuyu
item.description: Supporters of Mr. Lai, a Hong Kong dissident, and Mr. Dong, a Chinese journalist, hope President Trump will raise their cases with President Xi Jinping.
item.dc:creator: Catie Edmondson
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

media:content.media:credit: Lam Yik Fei for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Jimmy Lai at his home in Hong Kong in 2020. He was sentenced in February to 20 years in prison after Beijing blamed the Hong Kong publisher for leading the city’s major protests nearly seven years ago.

item.title: Live Updates: China’s Xi Warns Trump on Taiwan at Beijing Summit
item.description: Xi Jinping, China’s leader, told President Trump that Taiwan, if handled poorly, could lead to a clash with the United States. The two leaders are also expected to discuss trade and the Iran war at the two-day summit.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Canada and Alberta Reach Agreement to Raise Carbon Prices as Pipeline Tradeoff
item.description: But the agreement is the latest softening of carbon reduction measures introduced by Prime Minister Mark Carney since he took office last year.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen

media:content.media:credit: Pat Kane for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Syncrude facility near Fort McMurray, Alberta, producing synthetic crude oil from oil sands.

item.title: House Approves Year-Round Sale of Higher-Ethanol Gas
item.description: In a rare bipartisan vote, the House passed a bill allowing for the sale of an ethanol blend known as E15, over the objections of Republican hard-liners and some oil refiners.
item.dc:creator: Linda Qiu

media:content.media:credit: Jordan Gale for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: An ethanol processing plant in Wellsburg, Iowa, in 2023.

item.title: Giuliani Returns to Broadcasting After Illness: ‘I Feel 100 Percent’
item.description: Rudolph W. Giuliani, the 81-year-old former New York City mayor, was in critical condition with pneumonia in a Florida hospital earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Jonah E. Bromwich

media:content.media:description: Rudolph W. Giuliani, who led New York City in the aftermath of the Sept. 11 terrorist attacks, later became a close ally and personal lawyer to President Trump.

item.title: Second Venezuelan Doctor Is Released From Immigration Custody
item.description: At least five foreign-born doctors have been detained, according to a medical organization, raising fears that underserved rural areas reliant on them could be further strained.
item.dc:creator: Miriam Jordan

item.title: The China Gambit: From Nixon to Trump
item.description: President Trump’s visit to Beijing comes as relations with China have become more combative.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong

media:content.media:description: President Richard M. Nixon visiting Beijing in 1972.

item.title: Man Convicted of Running Illegal Police Station Tied to China’s Government
item.description: Prosecutors say that Lu Jianwang opened a hub in Manhattan’s Chinatown to monitor outspoken critics of the Chinese Communist Party.
item.dc:creator: Santul Nerkar
item.category: Ministry of Public Security of the People's Republic of China

media:content.media:credit: Jefferson Siegel for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Lu Jianwang was found guilty by a jury in federal court of acting as a foreign agent while he ran an illegal police station tied to the Chinese government.
